Class BinaryFeature

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.prover.strategy.costbased.feature.BinaryFeature
All Implemented Interfaces:
Feature
Direct Known Subclasses:
AllowedCutPositionFeature, AutomatedRuleFeature, BelowBinderFeature, BinaryTacletAppFeature, CompareCostsFeature, CutHeapObjectsFeature, DependencyContractFeature, FocusInAntecFeature, FormulaAddedByRuleFeature, ImplicitCastNecessary, NotInScopeOfModalityFeature, SeqContainsExecutableCodeFeature, SortComparisonFeature, SplittableQuantifiedFormulaFeature, ThrownExceptionFeature
public abstract class BinaryFeature extends Object implements Feature
Abstract superclass for features that have either zero cost or top cost.

  • Method Details

    • computeCost

      public <Goal extends ProofGoal<@NonNull Goal>> RuleAppCost computeCost(RuleApp app, PosInOccurrence pos, Goal goal, MutableState mState)
      Description copied from interface: Feature
      Evaluate the cost of a RuleApp.
      Specified by:
      computeCost in interface Feature
      Parameters:
      app - the RuleApp
      pos - position where app is to be applied
      goal - the goal on which app is to be applied
      mState -

      variable bank / local storage for feature who might require to store temporary information that changes during computation, e.g.

      invalid reference 
      TermBuffer
      s
      Returns:
      the cost of the rule application expressed as a RuleAppCost object. TopRuleAppCost.INSTANCE indicates that the rule shall not be applied at all (it is discarded by the strategy).

    • filter

      protected abstract <Goal extends ProofGoal<@NonNull Goal>> boolean filter(RuleApp app, PosInOccurrence pos, Goal goal, MutableState mState)
      Compute whether the result of the feature is zero (true) or infinity (false)
      Parameters:
      app - the RuleApp
      pos - position where app is to be applied
      goal - the goal on which app is to be applied
      mState - mutable state needed for feature computation
      Returns:
      true iff the result of the feature is supposed to be zero.
